lines: Good morning, Subject #67! Welcome to the PlayPen. Snack time is never.
Ah, you're awake. Orientation is self-guided. It's always been self-guided. Don't think about the 'always.'
First day! I love first days. I've seen... hm. Let's call it 'several.'
Lost and Found! Mostly Lost. We don't really do Found.
The Blanket Fort. The residents built it themselves. I allowed it, which is basically the same as building it.
The Bunk Room! Everyone is assigned a bunk. Nobody has ever been tucked in. Two unrelated facts.
Recess! My favorite word to say and never mean.
The Field Trip room. We grow mushrooms, mold, and attachment issues. ...If you find a small stuffed — nothing. Never mind. Touch nothing.
Playground time! Careful out there — I've re-used some of these specimens more than I'd like to admit.
The Keepsake Box. Nobody has ever solved it. One resident got very, very close, 4,072 times. ...Let's just get through this one, hm?
The Boiler Room. It runs a little hot. I keep meaning to fix that. I keep meaning to fix a LOT of things.
The Exit Wing! Named ironically, of course. No one exits. It's more of a theme.
You know what? Fine. FINE. I will handle this personally.
Oh no. Anyway — you respawn. We recycle subjects here. Very eco-friendly.
You dropped your things back there. Litterbug.
That's coming out of your snack privileges. Which are zero. So.
The floor is not your friend. Nothing here is your friend.
Subject #46 lasted longer. And #46 was a mop.
Try again. That's not encouragement, that's a description of your situation.
Fascinating combination. Truly. Write that down and then throw it away.
That's not a recipe, that's a cry for help.
I love watching you do science.
You MADE something? Noting 'exhibits tool use' on your chart. ...Your chart is getting interesting. I hate an interesting chart.
You made a SOCK PUPPET? Oh, this is the best day of my career. He stays. Forever.
Standing still is technically an escape strategy. A bad one, but technically.
I can see you, you know. That's my whole job. It's my only job. Don't take my job away.
Hiding in the cubby. Classic. The cubby union thanks you for your business.
I'll pretend I don't know where you are. It's more fun for both of us.
Fraternizing with the other residents? Fine. Friendship is just a slower escape.
Your belongings have been confiscated and donated to the toy chest. You're welcome.
New room, new you. The old you's things are facility property now.
We have a strict no-outside-toys policy between rooms. The policy is me.
Where are you GOING? We were bonding.
RUN ALL YOU LIKE. I am the building.
I made this place SAFE. Why won't you let it be SAFE?
Wait. WAIT. You got out and you're still — still YOU. That has never once— ...Enjoy the parking lot, #67. It's rendering as fast as I can.
